title: Myda Forge

{{Quote|I didn't come here willingly, but I did stay because I wanted to be with you.|Myda Forge to Kassar Forge|<ref name="WG" />}}
'''Myda Forge''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who had been sentenced to labor in the [[Spice mines of Kessel/Legends|spice mines]] of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. During her time there, she reformed her life and became a teacher who worked on a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] program on that world whose objective was to educate inmates on how to re-enter society upon their release. She was also the mother of [[Lujayne Forge|Lujayne]] and [[Inyri Forge]], who both became [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|Fear is as much a part of life on Kessel as spice or air. Control it and you’ll do fine|Myda Forge|<ref name="WG" />}}
[[File:Lujayne-forge-jpn.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Myda's first daughter, Lujayne.]]
Myda was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] inmate on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]], the infamous prison world, when she met [[Kassar Forge]], a teacher who instructed the inmates with the hopes of preventing them from returning to a life of crime upon their release. Although specifics about her past crimes are unclear, Myda was reformed and fell in love with Kassar. The two were [[Marriage/Legends|married]] and, although Kessel was not where she would have chosen to live otherwise, she remained with her husband and also began teaching there.<ref name="WG" />

The two spent the rest of their lives on the planet, rejecting all opportunities to leave, including an offer by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] in [[6 ABY/Legends|6.5 ABY]], who was freeing high-profile prisoners to help disrupt [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] operations on the galactic capital [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in the [[Standard month|months]] leading up to its [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|liberation]].<ref name="WG" />

Kassar and Myda had several sons and daughters, most of whom remained on the planet with them. Among their children were [[Lujayne Forge|Lujayne]] and [[Inyri Forge|Inyri]], both of whom went on to serve as [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] in [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], the elite [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] unit of the [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|New Republic Defense Force]].<ref name="WG" />

In 6.5 ABY, Myda and her family were devastated by the loss of her daughter, Lujayne, who was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in her sleep by Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] during a surprise covert assault on [[Talasea]].<ref name="RS">''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]''</ref>

She was only slightly less troubled when her younger daughter, Inyri, became the "[[Glitterstim/Legends|glitterstim]] [[cutter]]" and lover to [[Zekka Thyne]], a dangerous [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] criminal, who she insisted upon leaving Kessel with when he was selected by Antilles to be taken to Coruscant.<ref name="WG" />

Myda was more adamant than her husband regarding her disapproval of the relationship, and she implored Wedge to separate the two when he took Zekka, claiming she had seen people end up prisoners for making such bad decisions. Kassar, however, insisted that Inyri had to be left to make those decisions on her own, much like the inmates he instructed were left to make their own paths after being released.<ref name="WG" />
